title: Amplicon Preparation for Illumina Sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7e4d8","text":"This protocoldetails the description of Amplicon Preparation for Illumina Sequencing.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3a2qf","text":"Sequencing:\n1. Pool the prepared libraries by molarity in proportions appropriate for the sequencing kit.\n2. Sequence using an appropriate Illumina NGS sequencing kit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"atsa2","text":"Safety warnings:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hb1u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"79m0s","text":"Use IBI Isolate and Chloroform in a fume hood. Wear appropriate personal protective equipment. Dispose of hazardous materials appropriately.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Adapted from IBI Tri-Isolate Instruction Manual . All changes italics. Sample Homogenization and Lysis
Resuspend Beads should be in IBI Isolate and water.
RNA Binding.
Briefly vortex sample after thawing on bench.
Use magnetic rack to separate beads from liquid or centrifuge at 12000 x g, °C, - 16000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 . Transfer the clear supernatant to a new 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube (RNase-free).
Add 140 µL of chloroform and shake the tube vigorously for 00:00:10 then let stand for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Centrifuge at 12000 x g, °C, - 16000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 at 4 °C (then heat the centrifuge to Room temperature if used for the following steps).
Transfer ALL of the upper aqueous phase to a new 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube (RNase-free).
Add an equal volume of absolute ethanol and mix well by vortex.
Place a RB Column in a 2 mL Collection Tube and transfer all of the sample mixture to the RB Column.
Centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, - 16000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 then discard the flow-through.
Place the RB Column in a new 2 mL Collection Tube. SKIP Optional Step 1: In Column DNase I Digestion
RNA Wash.
Add 400 µL of Pre-Wash Buffer (make sure ethanol was added) to the RB Column then centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, - 16000 x g, °C, for 00:00:30 .
Discard the flow-through then place the RB Column back in the 2 mL Collection Tube.
Add 600 µL of Wash Buffer (make sure ethanol was added) to the RB Column.
Centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, - 16000 x g, °C, for 00:00:30 then discard the flow-through. Place the RB Column back in the 2 mL Collection Tube.
Add 600 µL of Wash Buffer (make sure ethanol was added) to the RB Column.
Centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, - 16000 x g, °C, for 00:00:30 then discard the flow-through.
Place the RB Column back in the 2 mL Collection Tube.
Centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, - 16000 x g, °C, for 00:03:00 to dry the column matrix.
RNA Elution.
Place the dry RB Column in a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube (RNase-free).
Add 53 µL of RNase-free Water into the CENTER of the column matrix to yield 50 µL of water after elution.
Let stand for at least 00:03:00 to ensure the RNase-free Water is completely absorbed by the matrix.
Centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, - 16000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 to elute the purified RNA.
Either immediately proceed to clean & concentrate or store at -80 °C .

Adapted from RNA Clean & Concentrator-5 Protocol . All changes in italics. Perform all steps at Room temperature and centrifugation at 10000 x g, °C, - 16000 x g, °C, for 00:00:30 , unless specified. Buffer Preparation.
Add 96 mL 100% ethanol ( 104 mL of 95% ethanol) to the 24 mL RNA Wash Buffer concentrate.
Total RNA Clean-up.
Add 2 volumes RNA Binding Buffer to each sample and mix. To minimize pipetting error, adjust the sample volume to 50 µL (minimum).
Add an equal volume of ethanol (95-100%) and mix.
Transfer the sample to the Zymo-Spin‱ IC Column in a Collection Tube and centrifuge. Discard the flow-through. To process samples >700 µL, Zymo-Spin‱ columns may be reloaded.
Add 400 µL RNA Prep Buffer to the column and centrifuge. Discard the flow-through.
Add 700 µL RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge. Discard the flow-through.
Add 400 µL RNA Wash Buffer to the column and centrifuge for 00:01:00 ensure complete removal of the wash buffer. Carefully, transfer the column into a RNase-free tube (not provided).
Add 15 µL if using 5 µg column or 25 µL if using 25 µg column DNase/RNase-Free Water directly to the column matrix and centrifuge. The eluted RNA can be used immediately or stored frozen at -80 °C .
Adapted from SuperScript IV Reverse Transcriptase User Guide . Measure RNA concentration using Qubit RNA HS assay.
Determine reaction scale needs for mass of RNA going into RT reaction.
Calculate mass of RNA going into RT reaction. Use ½ elution volume, so 7.5 µL if eluted in 15 µL or 12.5 µL if eluted in 25 µL . [RNA (ng/μl)] * volume of RNA (μL) = mass of RNA (ng).
Calculate mass factor, or how much RNA is going into RT reaction compared to RT Optimization # 3 (ie. 320 ng ). Mass of RNA (ng) / 320ng = mass factor.
Calculate RT reaction scale when scaling factor is 5. RT reaction scale = mass factor * scaling factor

Calculate the following for the RT reaction: Volume of water (uL) = RT reaction scale * 7 - volume RNA. Volume of Primer MM (uL) = RT reaction scale *1.25. Volume of RT MM (uL) = RT reaction scale * 4.375. Total volume (uL) = volume of RNA + volume of water + volume of Primer MM + Volume of RT MM.
Each total RT reaction needs to be split into 50 µL - 55 µL aliquots in PCR strips, so for each sample calculate: number of RT reactions = total volume / 50-55uL.

Determine volumes needed for Primer Mastermix. Each sample requires equal volumes JJS1 ( 2 micromolar (µM) ) and dNTP ( 10 millimolar (mM) ), with a minimum of 0.625 µL each. Prepare enough Primer MM for volume calculated in 8d + 10%. Combine appropriate volumes of JJS1 and dNTP On ice .
For each sample, combine volume of concentrated RNA and calculated volume of Primer Mastermix.
Incubate at 75 °C for 00:05:00 , ramp down to 4 °C , then set On ice for at least 00:01:00 .

Determine volumes needed for RT Mastermix. For the RT Mastermix, DTT (0.1M), Ribolock RNase inhibitor, SSIV enzyme, and SSIV buffer are combined in a 1:1:1:4 ratio, with a minimum volume per component of 0.625 µL . Prepare enough RT MM for volume calculated in 8d + 10%. Combine appropriate volumes of reagents On ice . Vortex SSIV buffer before use. Promptly return DTT to freezer after thawing. It has a veryshort half life, even at 4 °C .
For each sample combine all annealed RNA with appropriate volume RT Mastermix.
Heat to 57.5 °C for 00:30:00 , 80 °C for 00:10:00 , and hold at 4 °C .
Either store cDNA at -20 °C or immediately move to clean & concentrate.
Use Zymo DNA clean & concentrate kit to purify RT reaction using 1:5 binding buffer.
Determine volume needed for PCR 1 MM. Split each RT reaction into 2 PCRs (to use the entire reaction). Per PCR.

Combine 6.25 µL RT reaction + 2.5 µL JJS8 ( 10 micromolar (µM) ) + 2.5 µL JJS9-JJS15 ( 10 micromolar (µM) ) + 13.75 µL nuclease-free water + 1.5 µL DMSO + 25 µL Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix.
Pool replicate reactions. Clean using 2.5:1 ratio of Sera-Mag beads:reaction or Zymo DNA Clean & Concentrate kit. Elute using 20 µL nuclease-free water.
Store samples at -20 °C or proceed to PCR #2.

Combine 18 µL of cleaned PCR #1 + 2 µL nuclease-free water + 2.5 µL JJS16-JJS23 ( 10 micromolar (µM) ) + 2.5 µL JJS24 ( 10 micromolar (µM) ) + 0.5 µL 10x Sybr gold + 25 µL Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix + 1.0 µL DMSO + nuclease-free water to 51.5 µL .
Pool amplicons based on final RFU values.
Cycle: 98 °C for 00:00:30 Repeat until RFU ~ 3,000 (10-15 cycles): 98 °C for 00:00:10 63 °C for 00:00:20 72 °C for 00:00:30 .
If pooling samples that each have the same reads allocated to them: C1 * V1 = C2 * V2C1 = lowest final RFU valueV1 = 45 µL (or the max volume)C2 = final RFU of sampleV2 = volume to pool in μL

If each sample has a different number of reads allocated to it, before pooling: Ra = least reads allocated Va = V2 of the sample with the least reads allocated (Ra) For a given sample: Rb = reads allocated to sample Vb = volume to pool based on RFU and reads allocated in μL Vb = Va * (Rb/Ra) If Vb is >max volume: Vx = greatest Vb Vmax = 45 µL (or max volume) Vy = volume to pool based on RFU and reads allocated, adjusted to max volume, in μL *Vy = (Vmax/Vx)Vb Summary: if all samples have same reads allocated, pool V2. If all samples have different reads allocated and Vb≤Vmax, pool Vb, if Vb>Vmax, pool Vy. Example: C1 is bolded in column 2. Ra is bolded in column 4. %s %s
Separate amplicons by gel electrophoresis. Excise band at ~220bp. MAKE SURE TO SEPARATE AMPLICON FROM PRIMER DIMER! There is typically significant primer dimer.
Extract amplicons using Freeze ‘N Squeeze‱ DNA Gel Extraction Spin Columns.
Concentrate using Zymo DNA clean and concentrate columns, eluting into ~ 20 µL nuclease-free water.
Quantify using Qubit‱ dsDNA HS Assay Kit.
Store at -20 °C .
creator: Melinda K. Wheelock
